description.summary: The World Serpent of Norse mythology, enormous sea serpent encircling the entire Earth (Midgard), biting its own tail. Son of Loki, destined to fight Thor at Ragnarök in mutual destruction.
description.full: Jörmungandr (the Midgard Serpent or World Serpent) is an enormous sea serpent in Norse mythology, so large it encircles the entire world (Midgard) and grasps its own tail. Son of Loki and the jötunn Angrboða, brother to Fenrir and Hel, Jörmungandr was thrown into the ocean by Odin as a child and grew to vast size. The serpent is the arch-enemy of Thor, the thunder god. They encountered each other twice before their final battle: during Thor's fishing trip (where Thor nearly caught the serpent), and at Útgarða-Loki (where the serpent was disguised as a cat). At Ragnarök, Jörmungandr will emerge from the ocean, poison the sky, and engage Thor in their destined mutual destruction—Thor will kill the serpent but die from its venom after taking nine steps. Jörmungandr represents cosmic order and boundaries, similar to the ouroboros symbol of eternal cycles. The World Serpent embodies the contained chaos of the ocean and the limits of the world itself.

physical.appearance: Enormous serpent encircling the entire world
physical.size: So large it encircles Midgard (Earth) and bites own tail

features.description: Encircles the entire world, grasping tail in mouth
Poison deadly enough to kill Thor
Dwells in the ocean, part of aquatic realm

physical.morphology: Massive serpent forming a circle around the world

abilities.description: Large enough to surround entire Earth
At Ragnarök, poisons the sky with venom
Can be magically disguised (as cat by Útgarða-Loki)

behavior.disposition: hostile to Thor, maintaining cosmic boundaries
behavior.temperament: Enemy of Thor; represents contained chaos
notable_acts: Cast into ocean by Odin as infant
Nearly caught by Thor on fishing expedition
Disguised as cat, lifted by Thor at Útgarða-Loki
Destined to kill and be killed by Thor at Ragnarök

origin_story.birth: Born to Loki and Angrboða in Jötunheimr

siblings.name: Fenrir
siblings.description: Monstrous wolf destined to kill Odin

origin_story.context: Odin threw the infant serpent into the ocean, where it grew to world-encircling size

major_myths.title: Cast into the Ocean
major_myths.description: Odin took Loki's three monstrous children and dealt with each: Hel to the underworld, Fenrir bound in Asgard, and Jörmungandr thrown into the ocean surrounding Midgard. There the serpent grew until it encircled the entire world and grasped its own tail.

major_myths.title: Thor's Fishing Trip
major_myths.description: Thor went fishing with the jötunn Hymir using an ox head as bait. Jörmungandr took the bait, and Thor hauled the serpent up. As Thor prepared to strike with Mjölnir, Hymir cut the line in fear, and the serpent sank back into the sea. This was their first confrontation.

major_myths.title: The Cat of Útgarða-Loki
major_myths.description: When Thor visited the hall of Útgarða-Loki, he was challenged to lift a cat. Despite Thor's immense strength, he could barely lift one paw off the ground. Revealed later: the 'cat' was Jörmungandr magically disguised. Thor lifting even part of the World Serpent was an incredible feat.

major_myths.title: Ragnarök: Mutual Destruction
major_myths.description: At Ragnarök, Jörmungandr will release its tail and emerge from the ocean, thrashing and causing tidal waves. It will poison the sky with its venom. Thor will kill the serpent with Mjölnir, but he will take only nine steps before dying from the serpent's poison.

symbolism.explanation: Encircling the world represents boundaries and order
Biting own tail symbolizes eternal return, similar to ouroboros
Ocean's chaos held within world's boundaries

mythology.cultural_significance: Jörmungandr embodies the Norse understanding of cosmic boundaries, the contained chaos of the ocean, and the cyclical nature of destruction and renewal. The mutual destruction with Thor represents the end of the old order.

cultural_range.spread: Across Norse cultural influence including British Isles, Iceland, Greenland

mythological_location.name: The ocean surrounding Midgard
mythological_location.description: Jörmungandr dwells in the sea that encircles the world of humans
mythological_location.significance: Represents world boundaries and cosmic order

origins.cultural_roots: Norse/Germanic mythology; part of Loki's monstrous children and Ragnarök
origins.earliest_sources: Poetic Edda (13th century from earlier oral tradition)
origins.development: Central to Thor mythology and end-times prophecy

timeline.significance: Thor vs. serpent depicted in Viking Age art and poetry
Jörmungandr in fantasy media, games, popular culture

games: God of War Ragnarök (2022) - central role
other: Symbol of Norse mythology and cosmic cycles

cultural.modern_usage: Symbol of cosmic boundaries, eternal cycles, and the relationship between order and chaos. Popular in Norse revival movements and fantasy media.

sources.title: Poetic Edda
sources.description: Primary sources including Hymiskviða (fishing trip) and Völuspá (Ragnarök)

sources.title: Norse Mythology: A Guide to Gods, Heroes, Rituals, and Beliefs
